description: The SkinnedMeshRenderer component is used for rendering animated/dynamic 3D meshes in the world, and applying materials to that mesh.

fields.Mesh: The mesh to be rendered. Can be a StaticMesh or a Procedural Mesh
fields.Materials: A list of materials to be applied to the mesh
fields.MaterialPropertyBlocks: A list of material property blocks to apply to the materials on this mesh. Usually used for performance reasons where using multiple similar materials would take more resources.
fields.ShadowCastMode: How this object will cast shadows onto the world, or if it only draws a shadow.
fields.SortingOrder: Whether to render this before or after other renderers with geometry in the same location.
fields.BoundsComputeMethod: How the bounds of this mesh will be calculated. Should be left as Static if possible, for performance reasons.
fields.ProxyBoundsSource: A SkinnedMeshRenderer to use the ``ExplicitLocalBounds`` of rather than the ``ExplicitLocalBounds`` on this component.
fields.ExplicitLocalBounds: A box that within view will render the mesh, and when out of view will cull the mesh and stop rendering it. Is used when ``ProxyBoundsSource`` is null.
fields.Bones: Automatically Assigned — List of bones present in this mesh
fields.BlendShapeWeights: Automatically Assigned — List of blendshapes present in this mesh, and their respective weights.

methods.SplitBlenshapeAlongAxis: Splits a blendshape along a given axis, and returns a task representing if the operation is done yet.
methods.BakeBlendshape: bakes a blendshape to make it part of the mesh rest state, and returns a task representing if the operation is done yet.
methods.RemoveBlendshape: removes a blendshape from the mesh, and returns a task representing if the operation is done yet.
methods.VisualizeApproximateBoneBounds: Makes a series of boxes showing the bounds of the bones in the mesh.
methods.VisualizeBoneBounds: Makes a series of boxes showing the bounds of the bones in the mesh.
methods.SeparateOutBlendshapes: Separates out the blendshape affected mesh parts from the non blendshape affected mesh parts.
methods.StripEmptyBlendshapes: Removes blendshapes that don't change anything.
methods.BakeNonDrivenBlendshapes: Bakes all blendshapes on the mesh that aren't driven based on their current value, making it part of the rest state.
methods.BakeToStaticMesh: Bakes the mesh and makes it a static mesh instead.
methods.MergeBlendshapes: Combine all blendshapes in to one blendshape, which interpolates between the source blendshapes like a playback timeline.
methods.StripEmptyBones: Gets rid of bones that have no vertices assigned to them.
methods.ComputeExplicitBoundsFromPose: Computes the explicit bounds property for the skinned mesh renderer component based on it's current pose of it's bones.
methods.ExtendExplicitBoundsFromPose: extends the bounds of the explicit bounds property for the skinned mesh renderer component based on it's current pose of it's bones.
methods.SortBlendshapesByName: Sorts blendshapes by name in alphabetical order.
methods.SortBlendshapesByNameLength: Sorts blendshapes by how many characters are in a given blendshape name.
methods.SplitSubmeshes: Will split this mesh into additional submeshes, each having only one material
methods.MergeByMaterial: Will merge all submeshes that use the same material

usage: While it can be used for rendering static meshes, it is not recommended as there is a slight performance penalty for using SkinnedMeshRenderer, even if the animation features aren't used — Please try to use MeshRenderer where possible.
